violations.text: Veggie Cream Cheese was observed held beyond 7 days of the marked date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation and held for no longer than 7 days from the date of preparation unless frozen. Veggie Cream Cheese was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2025

At the time of inspection, several food items held in the front prep unit were observed held at 42 to 44 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Based on temperatures observed by employees at time of opening, Food items were moved to the walk-in cooler at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 10/25/2023

At the inspection, the establishment was lacking a thermometer to test food temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. New Violation. Correct By: 10/25/2023

At the time of inspection, the Sandwich Prep Unit was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature between 42 to 46 degrees Fahrenheit with food temps. Unit was adjusted at the time of inspection. Bulk food items are to be kept stored in the walk-in cooler and employees to work from small portions until unit can be verified as holding at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. PIC made call for service at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 10/25/2023
